headline: Fully verified bytecode with comprehensive audits from top-tier firms

findings.text: Core contracts including PoolManager (0x0000...8A90), PositionManager (0xbd21...ee9e), and Universal Router (0x66a9...8af) are verified on Etherscan and Sourcify as direct implementations.
The verified bytecode corresponds to the public 'Uniswap/v4-core' and 'Uniswap/v4-periphery' repositories. Core deployment matches the audited architecture.
Extensive audit coverage: Trail of Bits (July 2024), OpenZeppelin (July 2024), Spearbit (Sept 2024 for periphery, Oct 2025 for Kyber Hook), and Code4rena (Feb 2024).
All auditing firms (Trail of Bits, Spearbit, OpenZeppelin, Code4rena) are top-tier and broadly recognized in the Solidity security community.
Post-audit drift is effectively zero for the core PoolManager as it is immutable. Periphery and early production hooks (Kyber FairFlow) were audited through late 2025.

steelman.red: The modularity of the hook system means that while the core is audited, individual pools may use unverified or unaudited hook bytecode.
steelman.orange: The July 2024 core audits slightly predated the Jan 2025 mainnet launch, though the immutable singleton design minimizes the risk of intermediate drift.
steelman.green: The entire core protocol and standard periphery are verified, open-source, and have undergone multiple rigorous reviews by the industry's most reputable firms.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the Uniswap V4 core architecture is fully verified, open-source, and backed by a comprehensive audit trail from Tier-1 firms covering both the core singleton and the periphery positions manager.

evidence.shows: PoolManager bytecode is verified on Sourcify/Etherscan.
Trail of Bits core protocol audit (July 2024) covering singleton and flash accounting.
Spearbit periphery audit (Sept 2024) covering PositionManager and Universal Router integration.

protocol_metadata.about: Uniswap V4 is a non-custodial AMM featuring a singleton architecture and 'hooks' for custom pool logic. It uses flash accounting to optimize multi-hop swaps and liquidity management within a single contract.
